text.title: Experiment Event Tracking — Schema and Exposure Deduplication
text.summary: ExperimentEvent typed schema, ExperimentTracker with in-process exposure deduplication, and exposure vs conversion call sites.

rule.statement: Model every experiment event as a typed dataclass with experiment_id, variant, user_id, event_type (exposure | conversion | metric), metric_name, metric_value, timestamp, and properties.
rule.rationale: An untyped dict passed to the analytics client drifts over time; a dataclass is a code-reviewable schema that fails fast when fields are missing.

rule.statement: Deduplicate exposures by (experiment_id, user_id) within a request/session; never fire multiple exposure events for the same user in the same experiment during one page load.
rule.rationale: Duplicate exposures inflate the exposure count, causing sample-ratio mismatch and underestimating conversion rate.

rule.statement: In-process deduplication (_exposures set) is for development only; in production, persist the deduplication state to a durable store (Redis or a warehouse exposure table).
rule.rationale: In-process sets are lost on pod restart; in a multi-replica deployment each pod builds its own set, allowing duplicates across replicas.

rule.statement: Track exposure when the variant is rendered, not when assignment happens; track conversion on the specific downstream event — never at the same call site as exposure.
rule.rationale: Logging exposure and conversion at the same site conflates assignment with outcome. Analysis must join exposure → subsequent conversion; co-located calls destroy the temporal relationship.
